titleStmt.title: SII 28.222: original edition by J. Sundaram
No. 222. (A.R. No. 377 of 1913.)

summary.p: LĀLĀPĒṬṬAI, CHIDAMBARAM TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
Detached fragments built into the waste weir of the Vīrāṇam tank.

Fragmentary and incomplete. One fragment contains portions of the meykīrtti, beginning with Tiru-maṉṉi-vaḷara of Rājēndra-chōḻa I and seems to record a royal order. It refers to one Teṉṉavan viḻu ••••• whom the king mentions (as dear) as his son and probably to a grant for food-offerings to a deity. A second fragment seems to be dated in the reign of a king who took Madurai and the crowned head of the Pāṇḍya, (Kulōttuṅga III ?) who seems to be part of a record of a grant of some donotion for a sandhi lamp by a brahmin lady.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
ṭakkamum porukaṭalliḻattaraiyar tammuṭiyum Āṅkavar teviyar roṅkeḻil
ṇ karuti Iruttiya cempoṟṟiruttaku muṭiyum payaṅkoṭu paḻimika muyaṅkiyil mutu
kara nāyaṟṟu nāyaṟṟukkiḻamai peṟṟa makatti nānṟu nam makanār tenṉavan viḻu
nellu Eṇkalamāka van=ta nellu 2 100 5 10 6 kalam Innellu 2 100 5 10
ceytapaṭi potu tiruvamutarici � Āka nālu potaikkum
ppatakkum ne Another fragment reads as follows:
1. raiyum pāṇṭiyaṉ mu ••• ••••
2. śvaramuṭaiyārkku oru canti ••• ••••
3. varaṉbra rāmaṇi ā ••• •••• In characters of 12-13 century.
Digital edition of SII 28.222 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
